rationale: In the citing paper, Zhu and Meyers-Levy (2009) appears only as part of a grouped citation used to support a general background claim that interdependent consumers are more willing to sacrifice resources for close others. The paper does not discuss that study's specific context-effects mechanism, display-fixture setting, or findings in any distinct way, and it does not build its design or hypotheses directly on that article. This indicates a passing literature-support role rather than a meaningful directional dependency.
